120	func (s *OrderStore) listByField(ctx context.Context, field string, id uuid.UUID, page, pageSize int) ([]model.Order, int, error) {
121		var total int
122		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
123			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE `+field+` = $1`, id).Scan(&total)
124		if err != nil {
125			return nil, 0, err
126		}
127	
128		offset := (page - 1) * pageSize
129		rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
130			`SELECT id, idempotency_key, buyer_id, collectible_id, seller_id, status,
131			        price_snapshot_cents, cancellation_reason, cancelled_by, fulfillment_tracking,
132			        created_at, updated_at
133			 FROM orders WHERE `+field+` = $1
134			 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3`, id, pageSize, offset)
135		if err != nil {
136			return nil, 0, err
137		}
138		defer rows.Close()
139	
140		var orders []model.Order
141		for rows.Next() {
142			var o model.Order
143			if err := rows.Scan(&o.ID, &o.IdempotencyKey, &o.BuyerID, &o.CollectibleID, &o.SellerID,
144				&o.Status, &o.PriceSnapshotCents, &o.CancellationReason, &o.CancelledBy,
145				&o.FulfillmentTracking, &o.CreatedAt, &o.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
146				return nil, 0, err
147			}
148			orders = append(orders, o)
149		}
150		return orders, total, nil
151	}

157			buyerID).Scan(&count)
158		return count, err
159	}

161	func (s *OrderStore) CountCancelledInPeriod(ctx context.Context, userID uuid.UUID, hours int) (int, error) {
162		var count int
163		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
164			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders
165			 WHERE (buyer_id = $1 OR seller_id = $1)
166			   AND status = 'cancelled'
167			   AND updated_at > NOW() - make_interval(hours => $2)`,
168			userID, hours).Scan(&count)
169		return count, err
170	}
171	
172	func (s *OrderStore) BeginTx(ctx context.Context) (pgx.Tx, error) {
173		return s.pool.Begin(ctx)
174	}
175	
176	func (s *OrderStore) CountByStatus(ctx context.Context) (map[string]int64, error) {
177		rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
178			`SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM orders GROUP BY status`)
179		if err != nil {
180			return nil, err
181		}
182		defer rows.Close()
183	
184		result := make(map[string]int64)
185		for rows.Next() {
186			var status string
187			var count int64
188			if err := rows.Scan(&status, &count); err != nil {
189				return nil, err
190			}
191			result[status] = count
192		}
193		return result, nil
194	}

1	package worker
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
8		"go.uber.org/zap"
9	)
10	
11	func AnalyticsRollupJob(pool *pgxpool.Pool, logger *zap.Logger) Job {
12		return Job{
13			Name:     "analytics_rollup",
14			Interval: 1 * time.Hour,
15			Fn: func(ctx context.Context) error {
16				// Pre-aggregate funnel data (views and orders) for 7-day and 30-day windows
17				_, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
18					INSERT INTO analytics_rollups (period_days, views, orders, conversion_rate, computed_at)
19					SELECT
20						7,
21						COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0),
22						COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'order_created' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0),
23						CASE
24							WHEN COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) > 0
25							THEN COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'order_created' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0)::numeric
26								/ COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0)::numeric
27							ELSE 0
28						END,
29						NOW()
30					FROM analytics_events
31					WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days'
32					ON CONFLICT (period_days) DO UPDATE
33						SET views = EXCLUDED.views,
34							orders = EXCLUDED.orders,
35							conversion_rate = EXCLUDED.conversion_rate,
36							computed_at = EXCLUDED.computed_at
37				`)
38				if err != nil {
39					logger.Error("rollup 7-day funnel failed", zap.Error(err))
40				}
41	
42				_, err = pool.Exec(ctx, `
43					INSERT INTO analytics_rollups (period_days, views, orders, conversion_rate, computed_at)
44					SELECT
45						30,
46						COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0),
47						COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'order_created' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0),
48						CASE
49							WHEN COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) > 0
50							THEN COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'order_created' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0)::numeric
51								/ COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'view' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0)::numeric
52							ELSE 0
53						END,
54						NOW()
55					FROM analytics_events
56					WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
57					ON CONFLICT (period_days) DO UPDATE
58						SET views = EXCLUDED.views,
59							orders = EXCLUDED.orders,
60							conversion_rate = EXCLUDED.conversion_rate,
61							computed_at = EXCLUDED.computed_at
62				`)
63				if err != nil {
64					logger.Error("rollup 30-day funnel failed", zap.Error(err))
65				}
66	
67				// Pre-aggregate retention cohorts for the last 30 days
68				_, err = pool.Exec(ctx, `
69					INSERT INTO retention_rollups (cohort_date, cohort_size, retained_count, retention_rate, computed_at)
70					WITH cohorts AS (
71						SELECT user_id, DATE(MIN(created_at)) AS cohort_date
72						FROM analytics_events
73						WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
74						GROUP BY user_id
75					),
76					activity AS (
77						SELECT DISTINCT user_id, DATE(created_at) AS activity_date
78						FROM analytics_events
79						WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
80					)
81					SELECT
82						c.cohort_date,
83						COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id)::int AS cohort_size,
84						COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN a.activity_date > c.cohort_date THEN a.user_id END)::int AS retained,
85						CASE
86							WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) > 0
87							THEN COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN a.activity_date > c.cohort_date THEN a.user_id END)::numeric
88								/ COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id)::numeric
89							ELSE 0
90						END AS retention_rate,
91						NOW()
92					FROM cohorts c
93					LEFT JOIN activity a ON c.user_id = a.user_id
94					GROUP BY c.cohort_date
95					ON CONFLICT (cohort_date) DO UPDATE
96						SET cohort_size = EXCLUDED.cohort_size,
97							retained_count = EXCLUDED.retained_count,
98							retention_rate = EXCLUDED.retention_rate,
99							computed_at = EXCLUDED.computed_at
100				`)
101				if err != nil {
102					logger.Error("rollup retention failed", zap.Error(err))
103				}
104	
105				logger.Info("analytics rollup completed")
106				return nil
107			},
108		}
109	}
110

1	package middleware
2	
3	import (
4		"net/http"
5	
6		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
7		"github.com/ledgermint/platform/internal/dto"
8	)
9	
10	// GlobalErrorHandler converts all Echo errors into consistent JSON format.
11	// This ensures no raw framework errors (HTML or plain text) leak to clients.
12	func GlobalErrorHandler(err error, c echo.Context) {
13		if c.Response().Committed {
14			return
15		}
16	
17		code := http.StatusInternalServerError
18		errCode := dto.CodeInternal
19		message := "internal server error"
20	
21		if he, ok := err.(*echo.HTTPError); ok {
22			code = he.Code
23	
24			// If the error message is already our ErrorResponse format, use it directly
25			if resp, ok := he.Message.(dto.ErrorResponse); ok {
26				c.JSON(code, resp)
27				return
28			}
29	
30			// Map common HTTP status codes to our error codes
31			switch code {
32			case http.StatusBadRequest:
33				errCode = dto.CodeValidation
34				message = "bad request"
35			case http.StatusUnauthorized:
36				errCode = dto.CodeUnauthorized
37				message = "unauthorized"
38			case http.StatusForbidden:
39				errCode = dto.CodeForbidden
40				message = "forbidden"
41			case http.StatusNotFound:
42				errCode = dto.CodeNotFound
43				message = "not found"
44			case http.StatusMethodNotAllowed:
45				errCode = dto.CodeValidation
46				message = "method not allowed"
47			case http.StatusTooManyRequests:
48				errCode = dto.CodeRateLimited
49				message = "rate limited"
50			case http.StatusServiceUnavailable:
51				errCode = dto.CodeInternal
52				message = "service unavailable"
53			}
54	
55			// Use the echo error message if it's a string
56			if msg, ok := he.Message.(string); ok && msg != "" {
57				message = msg
58			}
59		}
60	
61		c.JSON(code, dto.ErrorResponse{
62			Error: dto.ErrorDetail{
63				Code:      errCode,
64				Message:   message,
65				RequestID: GetRequestID(c),
66			},
67		})
68	}
69

1	"""Unit tests for order state machine transitions."""
2	import unittest
3	
4	
5	# Replicate the order state machine from backend/internal/model/order.go
6	ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS = {
7	    "pending": ["confirmed", "cancelled"],
8	    "confirmed": ["processing", "cancelled"],
9	    "processing": ["completed"],
10	    "completed": [],
11	    "cancelled": [],
12	}
13	
14	ALL_STATUSES = ["pending", "confirmed", "processing", "completed", "cancelled"]
15	
16	
17	def can_transition(from_status, to_status):
18	    return to_status in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(from_status, [])
19	
20	
21	class TestOrderStateMachine(unittest.TestCase):
22	    """Tests for order state transitions matching backend/internal/model/order.go."""
23	
24	    # --- Valid transitions ---
25	
26	    def test_pending_to_confirmed(self):
27	        self.assertTrue(can_transition("pending", "confirmed"))
28	
29	    def test_pending_to_cancelled(self):
30	        self.assertTrue(can_transition("pending", "cancelled"))
31	
32	    def test_confirmed_to_processing(self):
33	        self.assertTrue(can_transition("confirmed", "processing"))
34	
35	    def test_confirmed_to_cancelled(self):
36	        self.assertTrue(can_transition("confirmed", "cancelled"))
37	
38	    def test_processing_to_completed(self):
39	        self.assertTrue(can_transition("processing", "completed"))
40	
41	    # --- Invalid transitions ---
42	
43	    def test_pending_cannot_skip_to_processing(self):
44	        self.assertFalse(can_transition("pending", "processing"))
45	
46	    def test_pending_cannot_skip_to_completed(self):
47	        self.assertFalse(can_transition("pending", "completed"))
48	
49	    def test_processing_cannot_be_cancelled(self):
50	        self.assertFalse(can_transition("processing", "cancelled"))
51	
52	    def test_completed_is_terminal(self):
53	        for status in ALL_STATUSES:
54	            self.assertFalse(
55	                can_transition("completed", status),
56	                f"completed should not transition to {status}",
57	            )
58	
59	    def test_cancelled_is_terminal(self):
60	        for status in ALL_STATUSES:
61	            self.assertFalse(
62	                can_transition("cancelled", status),
63	                f"cancelled should not transition to {status}",
64	            )
65	
66	    def test_self_transitions_are_invalid(self):
67	        for status in ALL_STATUSES:
68	            self.assertFalse(
69	                can_transition(status, status),
70	                f"{status} should not transition to itself",
71	            )
72	
73	    def test_backward_transitions_invalid(self):
74	        self.assertFalse(can_transition("confirmed", "pending"))
75	        self.assertFalse(can_transition("processing", "confirmed"))
76	        self.assertFalse(can_transition("processing", "pending"))
77	        self.assertFalse(can_transition("completed", "processing"))
78	
79	    # --- Boundary conditions ---
80	
81	    def test_unknown_status_has_no_transitions(self):
82	        self.assertFalse(can_transition("unknown_status", "pending"))
83	
84	    def test_all_statuses_have_transition_entries(self):
85	        for status in ALL_STATUSES:
86	            self.assertIn(status, ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS)
87	
88	    def test_happy_path_full_lifecycle(self):
89	        """Verify the complete happy path: pending -> confirmed -> processing -> completed."""
90	        path = ["pending", "confirmed", "processing", "completed"]
91	        for i in range(len(path) - 1):
92	            self.assertTrue(
93	                can_transition(path[i], path[i + 1]),
94	                f"Should transition from {path[i]} to {path[i+1]}",
95	            )
96	
97	    def test_cancellation_path_from_pending(self):
98	        self.assertTrue(can_transition("pending", "cancelled"))
99	
100	    def test_cancellation_path_from_confirmed(self):
101	        self.assertTrue(can_transition("confirmed", "cancelled"))
102	
103	    def test_transition_targets_are_valid_statuses(self):
104	        for from_status, targets in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.items():
105	            for target in targets:
106	                self.assertIn(
107	                    target,
108	                    ALL_STATUSES,
109	                    f"Transition target '{target}' from '{from_status}' is not a valid status",
110	                )
111	
112	
113	if __name__ == "__main__":
114	    unittest.main()

1	"""Unit tests for PII detection logic matching frontend/src/utils/piiDetector.ts."""
2	import re
3	import unittest
4	
5	
6	# Replicate PII detection patterns from backend/internal/service/pii.go
7	SSN_REGEX = re.compile(r"\b\d{3}-?\d{2}-?\d{4}\b")
8	PHONE_REGEX = re.compile(r"\b(\+?1[-.\s]?)?\(?\d{3}\)?[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}\b")
9	EMAIL_REGEX = re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+\-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.\-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b")
10	
11	
12	def detect_pii(text):
13	    types = []
14	    if SSN_REGEX.search(text):
15	        types.append("SSN")
16	    if PHONE_REGEX.search(text):
17	        types.append("phone number")
18	    if EMAIL_REGEX.search(text):
19	        types.append("email address")
20	    return {"detected": len(types) > 0, "types": types}
21	
22	
23	class TestPIIDetection(unittest.TestCase):
24	    """Tests for PII detection matching frontend/src/utils/piiDetector.ts."""
25	
26	    # --- SSN Detection ---
27	
28	    def test_ssn_with_dashes(self):
29	        result = detect_pii("My SSN is 123-45-6789")
30	        self.assertTrue(result["detected"])
31	        self.assertIn("SSN", result["types"])
32	
33	    def test_ssn_without_dashes(self):
34	        result = detect_pii("SSN: 123456789")
35	        self.assertTrue(result["detected"])
36	        self.assertIn("SSN", result["types"])
37	
38	    def test_no_ssn_in_normal_text(self):
39	        result = detect_pii("This is a regular message about order #42")
40	        self.assertNotIn("SSN", result["types"])
41	
42	    def test_ssn_partial_dashes(self):
43	        result = detect_pii("Number is 12345-6789")
44	        # This matches the pattern as written
45	        self.assertTrue(result["detected"])
46	
47	    # --- Phone Number Detection ---
48	
49	    def test_phone_with_dashes(self):
50	        result = detect_pii("Call me at 555-123-4567")
51	        self.assertTrue(result["detected"])
52	        self.assertIn("phone number", result["types"])
53	
54	    def test_phone_with_dots(self):
55	        result = detect_pii("Phone: 555.123.4567")
56	        self.assertTrue(result["detected"])
57	        self.assertIn("phone number", result["types"])
58	
59	    def test_phone_with_parens(self):
60	        result = detect_pii("Call (555) 123-4567")
61	        self.assertTrue(result["detected"])
62	        self.assertIn("phone number", result["types"])
63	
64	    def test_phone_with_country_code(self):
65	        result = detect_pii("Dial +1-555-123-4567")
66	        self.assertTrue(result["detected"])
67	        self.assertIn("phone number", result["types"])
68	
69	    def test_phone_with_spaces(self):
70	        result = detect_pii("Phone: 555 123 4567")
71	        self.assertTrue(result["detected"])
72	        self.assertIn("phone number", result["types"])
73	
74	    # --- No PII ---
75	
76	    def test_clean_message(self):
77	        result = detect_pii("Hello, when will my order ship?")
78	        self.assertFalse(result["detected"])
79	        self.assertEqual(result["types"], [])
80	
81	    def test_empty_string(self):
82	        result = detect_pii("")
83	        self.assertFalse(result["detected"])
84	        self.assertEqual(result["types"], [])
85	
86	    def test_order_id_not_detected(self):
87	        result = detect_pii("Order #10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001")
88	        # UUID should not match SSN pattern
89	        self.assertNotIn("SSN", result["types"])
90	
91	    def test_price_not_detected_as_ssn(self):
92	        result = detect_pii("The price is $99.99")
93	        self.assertNotIn("SSN", result["types"])
94	
95	    # --- Email Detection (server-side PII) ---
96	
97	    def test_email_detected(self):
98	        result = detect_pii("Email me at user@example.com")
99	        self.assertTrue(result["detected"])
100	        self.assertIn("email address", result["types"])
101	
102	    def test_email_with_plus(self):
103	        result = detect_pii("Contact user+tag@example.com")
104	        self.assertTrue(result["detected"])
105	        self.assertIn("email address", result["types"])
106	
107	    def test_email_not_in_normal_text(self):
108	        result = detect_pii("Hello, when will my order ship?")
109	        self.assertNotIn("email address", result["types"])
110	
111	    # --- Multiple PII types ---
112	
113	    def test_both_ssn_and_phone(self):

6	# Validation rules from dto/request.go
7	VALIDATION_RULES = {
8	    "username": {"min": 3, "max": 100, "required": True},
9	    "password": {"min": 8, "max": 128, "required": True},
10	    "display_name": {"max": 200, "required": True},
11	    "email": {"pattern": r"^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$", "required": False},
12	    "title": {"max": 300, "required": True},
13	    "description": {"max": 5000, "required": False},
14	    "currency": {"exact_len": 3, "required": False},
15	    "contract_address": {"max": 42, "required": False},
16	    "cancel_reason": {"max": 500, "required": True},
17	    "hide_reason": {"max": 500, "required": True},
18	    "message_body": {"max": 5000, "required": True},
19	    "role_name": {"allowed": ["buyer", "seller", "administrator", "compliance_analyst"], "required": True},
20	    "ip_action": {"allowed": ["allow", "deny"], "required": True},
21	    "traffic_pct": {"min": 1, "max": 100, "required": True},
22	    "rollback_threshold_pct": {"min": 1, "max": 100, "required": True},
23	    "price_cents": {"min": 1, "required": True},
24	}
25	
26	
27	def validate_length(value, rules):
28	    """Validate string length against rules."""
29	    if rules.get("required") and not value:
30	        return False
31	    if not value and not rules.get("required"):
32	        return True
33	    if "min" in rules and len(value) < rules["min"]:
34	        return False
35	    if "max" in rules and len(value) > rules["max"]:
36	        return False
37	    if "exact_len" in rules and len(value) != rules["exact_len"]:
38	        return False
39	    if "pattern" in rules and not re.match(rules["pattern"], value):
40	        return False
41	    if "allowed" in rules and value not in rules["allowed"]:
42	        return False
43	    return True
44	
45	
46	def validate_numeric(value, rules):
47	    """Validate numeric value against rules."""
48	    if rules.get("required") and value is None:
49	        return False
50	    if value is None and not rules.get("required"):
51	        return True
52	    if "min" in rules and value < rules["min"]:
53	        return False
54	    if "max" in rules and value > rules["max"]:
55	        return False
56	    return True
57

1	"""Unit tests for formatting utilities matching frontend/src/utils/formatters.ts."""
2	import unittest
3	
4	
5	def format_cents(cents, currency="USD"):
6	    """Replicate formatCents from frontend/src/utils/formatters.ts."""
7	    amount = cents / 100
8	    if currency == "USD":
9	        return f"${amount:,.2f}"
10	    return f"{amount:,.2f} {currency}"
11	
12	
13	def mask_value(value):
14	    """Replicate maskValue from frontend/src/components/shared/MaskedField.tsx."""
15	    if "@" in value:
16	        parts = value.split("@")
17	        local = parts[0]
18	        domain = parts[1]
19	        last_dot = domain.rfind(".")
20	        return local[0] + "***@" + domain[0] + "***" + domain[last_dot:]
21	    if len(value) > 4:
22	        return "***" + value[-4:]
23	    return "***"
24	
25

149	    def test_order_confirmation_requires_seller(self):
150	        self.assertEqual(self.ROUTE_ROLES["/api/orders/:id/confirm"], ["seller"])
151	
152	    def test_analytics_allows_admin_and_compliance(self):
153	        roles = self.ROUTE_ROLES["/api/analytics"]
154	        self.assertIn("administrator", roles)
155	        self.assertIn("compliance_analyst", roles)
156	
157	    def test_ab_tests_admin_only(self):
158	        self.assertEqual(self.ROUTE_ROLES["/api/ab-tests"], ["administrator"])
159	
160	    def test_moderation_requires_admin(self):
161	        self.assertEqual(self.ROUTE_ROLES["/api/collectibles/:id/hide"], ["administrator"])
162	        self.assertEqual(self.ROUTE_ROLES["/api/collectibles/:id/publish"], ["administrator"])
163	
164	    def test_anomalies_allows_admin_and_compliance(self):
165	        roles = self.ROUTE_ROLES["/api/admin/anomalies"]
166	        self.assertIn("administrator", roles)
167	        self.assertIn("compliance_analyst", roles)
168	
169	
170	if __name__ == "__main__":
171	    unittest.main()
172

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Pre-flight check: verifies API is reachable and seed data is present.
3	# This script runs first (sorted alphabetically) so the suite fails
4	# immediately with a clear message instead of producing confusing
5	# 401/403 noise when seed users or collectibles are missing.
6	
7	BASE_URL="${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
8	PASS=0
9	FAIL=0
10	
11	assert_status() {
12	    local test_name="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3"
13	    if [ "$expected" = "$actual" ]; then
14	        echo "  PASS: $test_name (HTTP $actual)"
15	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
16	    else
17	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected HTTP $expected, got HTTP $actual)"
18	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
19	    fi
20	}
21	
22	echo "=== Pre-flight Setup Checks ==="
23	
24	# ---- 1. API reachability ----
25	echo ""
26	echo "--- API Reachability ---"
27	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" --max-time 5 \
28	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
29	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
30	    -d '{"username":"__probe__","password":"probeprobe"}' 2>/dev/null)
31	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
32	
33	if [ -z "$HTTP_CODE" ] || [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "000" ]; then
34	    echo "  FAIL: API is not reachable at $BASE_URL"
35	    echo ""
36	    echo "  Ensure services are running:"
37	    echo "    docker compose up -d"
38	    echo ""
39	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
40	    echo "=== Setup Checks Summary: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed ==="
41	    exit $FAIL
42	else
43	    echo "  PASS: API is reachable at $BASE_URL"
44	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
45	fi
46	
47	# ---- 2. Seed users exist ----
48	echo ""
49	echo "--- Seed Users ---"
50	
51	check_user() {
52	    local username="$1" role="$2"
53	    local resp http_code body
54	
55	    resp=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
56	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
57	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
58	        -d "{\"username\":\"$username\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}")
59	    http_code=$(echo "$resp" | tail -1)
60	    body=$(echo "$resp" | head -n -1)
61	
62	    if [ "$http_code" = "200" ]; then
63	        echo "  PASS: User '$username' exists and password is correct"
64	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
65	    else
66	        echo "  FAIL: User '$username' missing or wrong password (HTTP $http_code)"
67	        echo "        Seed data is required. Run:"
68	        echo "          docker compose exec postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -f /dev/stdin < scripts/seed.sql"
69	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
70	    fi
71	}
72	
73	check_user "admin"    "administrator"
74	check_user "seller1"  "seller"
75	check_user "buyer1"   "buyer"
76	check_user "analyst1" "compliance_analyst"
77	
78	# ---- 3. Seed collectibles exist ----
79	echo ""
80	echo "--- Seed Collectibles ---"
81	
82	# Log in as buyer to query collectibles (any authenticated user can list)
83	COOKIE_JAR=$(mktemp)
84	curl -s -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
85	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
86	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
87	    -d '{"username":"buyer1","password":"testpass123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
88	
89	CSRF_TOKEN=$(grep csrf_token "$COOKIE_JAR" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $NF}')
90	
91	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
92	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $CSRF_TOKEN" \
93	    "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" 2>/dev/null)
94	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
95	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
96	
97	if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ]; then
98	    TOTAL=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('total_count',0))" 2>/dev/null)
99	    if [ "$TOTAL" -ge 3 ] 2>/dev/null; then
100	        echo "  PASS: Seed collectibles present (count=$TOTAL)"
101	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
102	    else
103	        echo "  FAIL: Expected >= 3 seed collectibles, found $TOTAL"
104	        echo "        Run: docker compose exec postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -f /dev/stdin < scripts/seed.sql"
105	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
106	    fi
107	else
108	    echo "  FAIL: Could not list collectibles (HTTP $HTTP_CODE) — seed check skipped"
109	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
110	fi
111	
112	# Check specific collectible IDs used by other tests
113	for CID in "10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001" \
114	            "10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002" \
115	            "10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003"; do
116	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
117	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $CSRF_TOKEN" \
118	        "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles/$CID" 2>/dev/null)
119	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
120	    if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ]; then
121	        TITLE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1 | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['collectible']['title'])" 2>/dev/null)
122	        echo "  PASS: Collectible $CID exists ($TITLE)"
123	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
124	    else
125	        echo "  FAIL: Collectible $CID not found (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)"
126	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
127	    fi
128	done
129	
130	rm -f "$COOKIE_JAR"
131	
132	# ---- Summary ----
133	echo ""
134	if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
135	    echo "  *** SETUP FAILED — remaining API tests will likely produce false failures ***"
136	    echo "  Fix the issues above and re-run."
137	fi
138	echo "=== Setup Checks Summary: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed ==="
139	exit $FAIL
140

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# API tests for collectible endpoints
3	# Requires: services running, seed data loaded
4	
5	BASE_URL="${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
6	PASS=0
7	FAIL=0
8	
9	assert_status() {
10	    local test_name="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3"
11	    if [ "$expected" = "$actual" ]; then
12	        echo "  PASS: $test_name (HTTP $actual)"
13	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
14	    else
15	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected HTTP $expected, got HTTP $actual)"
16	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
17	    fi
18	}
19	
20	assert_json_field() {
21	    local test_name="$1" body="$2" field="$3" expected="$4"
22	    local actual
23	    actual=$(echo "$body" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d${field})" 2>/dev/null)
24	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then

35	    local actual
36	    actual=$(echo "$body" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d${field})" 2>/dev/null)
37	    if [ "$actual" -gt "$min_val" ] 2>/dev/null; then
38	        echo "  PASS: $test_name ($actual > $min_val)"
39	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
40	    else
41	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected > $min_val, got '$actual')"
42	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
43	    fi
44	}
45	
46	# Login as seller

139	fi

142	echo ""

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# API tests for message endpoints
3	# Requires: services running, seed data loaded, at least one order existing
4	
5	BASE_URL="${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
6	PASS=0
7	FAIL=0
8	
9	assert_status() {
10	    local test_name="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3"
11	    if [ "$expected" = "$actual" ]; then
12	        echo "  PASS: $test_name (HTTP $actual)"
13	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
14	    else
15	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected HTTP $expected, got HTTP $actual)"
16	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
17	    fi
18	}
19	
20	assert_json_field() {
21	    local test_name="$1" body="$2" field="$3" expected="$4"
22	    local actual
23	    actual=$(echo "$body" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d${field})" 2>/dev/null)
24	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
25	        echo "  PASS: $test_name"
26	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
27	    else
28	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected '$expected', got '$actual')"
29	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
30	    fi
31	}
32	
33	# Login as buyer
34	BUYER_JAR=$(mktemp)
35	curl -s -c "$BUYER_JAR" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
36	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
37	    -d '{"username":"buyer1","password":"testpass123"}' > /dev/null
38	BUYER_CSRF=$(grep csrf_token "$BUYER_JAR" | awk '{print $NF}')
39	
40	# Login as seller
41	SELLER_JAR=$(mktemp)
42	curl -s -c "$SELLER_JAR" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
43	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
44	    -d '{"username":"seller1","password":"testpass123"}' > /dev/null
45	SELLER_CSRF=$(grep csrf_token "$SELLER_JAR" | awk '{print $NF}')
46	
47	# Login as analyst (should not be able to send messages)
48	ANALYST_JAR=$(mktemp)
49	curl -s -c "$ANALYST_JAR" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
50	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
51	    -d '{"username":"analyst1","password":"testpass123"}' > /dev/null
52	ANALYST_CSRF=$(grep csrf_token "$ANALYST_JAR" | awk '{print $NF}')
53	
54	cleanup() { rm -f "$BUYER_JAR" "$SELLER_JAR" "$ANALYST_JAR"; }
55	trap cleanup EXIT
56	
57	echo "=== Messages API Tests ==="
58	
59	# First create an order to test messages on
60	COLLECTIBLE_ID="10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002"
61	MSG_IDEM=$(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")
62	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
63	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
64	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
65	    -H "Idempotency-Key: $MSG_IDEM" \
66	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders" \
67	    -d "{\"collectible_id\":\"$COLLECTIBLE_ID\"}")
68	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
69	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
70	ORDER_ID=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
71	
72	if [ -z "$ORDER_ID" ] || [ "$ORDER_ID" = "None" ] || [ "$ORDER_ID" = "" ]; then
73	    # Try to get from existing orders
74	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
75	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
76	        "$BASE_URL/api/orders?role=buyer")
77	    ORDER_ID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin)['data']; print(d[0]['id'] if d else '')" 2>/dev/null)
78	fi
79	
80	if [ -z "$ORDER_ID" ] || [ "$ORDER_ID" = "None" ] || [ "$ORDER_ID" = "" ]; then
81	    echo "  SKIP: No order available for message tests"
82	    echo "=== Messages Tests Summary: 0 passed, 0 failed (skipped) ==="
83	    exit 0
84	fi
85	
86	echo "  Using order: $ORDER_ID"
87	
88	# Test 1: Buyer sends message
89	echo ""
90	echo "--- Send Messages ---"
91	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
92	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
93	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$ORDER_ID/messages" \
94	    -F "body=Hello, when will this ship?")
95	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
96	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
97	assert_status "Buyer sends message (201)" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
98	
99	# Test 2: Seller sends message
100	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
101	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
102	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$ORDER_ID/messages" \
103	    -F "body=It will ship tomorrow!")
104	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
105	assert_status "Seller sends message (201)" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
106	
107	# Test 3: List messages
108	echo ""
109	echo "--- List Messages ---"
110	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
111	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
112	    "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$ORDER_ID/messages")
113	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
114	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
115	assert_status "List messages returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
116	
117	MSG_COUNT=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['total_count'])" 2>/dev/null)
118	if [ "$MSG_COUNT" -ge 2 ] 2>/dev/null; then
119	    echo "  PASS: Messages returned (count=$MSG_COUNT)"
120	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
121	else
122	    echo "  FAIL: Expected at least 2 messages, got $MSG_COUNT"
123	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
124	fi
125	
126	# Test 4: Send empty message
127	echo ""
128	echo "--- Message Validation ---"
129	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
130	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
131	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$ORDER_ID/messages" \
132	    -F "body=")
133	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
134	assert_status "Send empty message returns 422" "422" "$HTTP_CODE"
135	
136	# Test 5: Analyst cannot send message (not buyer/seller of order)
137	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ANALYST_JAR" \
138	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ANALYST_CSRF" \
139	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$ORDER_ID/messages" \
140	    -F "body=I should not be able to send this")
141	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
142	assert_status "Non-party cannot send message (403)" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
143	
144	# Test 6: Messages on nonexistent order
145	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
146	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
147	    "$BASE_URL/api/orders/00000000-0000-0000-0000-999999999999/messages")
148	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
149	assert_status "Messages on nonexistent order returns error" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
150	
151	echo ""
152	echo "=== Messages Tests Summary: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed ==="
153	exit $FAIL
154

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# API tests for authentication endpoints
3	# Requires: services running via docker compose up, seed data loaded
4	
5	BASE_URL="${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
6	PASS=0
7	FAIL=0
8	COOKIE_JAR=$(mktemp)
9	
10	cleanup() { rm -f "$COOKIE_JAR"; }
11	trap cleanup EXIT
12	
13	assert_status() {
14	    local test_name="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3"
15	    if [ "$expected" = "$actual" ]; then
16	        echo "  PASS: $test_name (HTTP $actual)"
17	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
18	    else
19	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected HTTP $expected, got HTTP $actual)"
20	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
21	    fi
22	}
23	
24	assert_json_field() {
25	    local test_name="$1" body="$2" field="$3" expected="$4"
26	    local actual
27	    actual=$(echo "$body" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d${field})" 2>/dev/null)
28	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
29	        echo "  PASS: $test_name"
30	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
31	    else
32	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected '$expected', got '$actual')"
33	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
34	    fi
35	}
36	
37	echo "=== Auth API Tests ==="
38	
39	# Test 1: Login with valid credentials
40	echo ""
41	echo "--- Login Tests ---"
42	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
43	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
44	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
45	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"testpass123"}')
46	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
47	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
48	assert_status "Login with valid admin credentials" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
49	assert_json_field "Login returns username" "$BODY" "['user']['username']" "admin"
50	assert_json_field "Login returns admin role" "$BODY" "['roles'][0]" "administrator"
51	
52	# Test 2: Login returns cookies
53	COOKIES=$(cat "$COOKIE_JAR")
54	if echo "$COOKIES" | grep -q "access_token"; then
55	    echo "  PASS: Login sets access_token cookie"
56	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
57	else
58	    echo "  FAIL: Login should set access_token cookie"
59	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
60	fi
61	
62	if echo "$COOKIES" | grep -q "csrf_token"; then
63	    echo "  PASS: Login sets csrf_token cookie"
64	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
65	else
66	    echo "  FAIL: Login should set csrf_token cookie"
67	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
68	fi
69	
70	# Test 3: Login with invalid password
71	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
72	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
73	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
74	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"wrongpassword"}')
75	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
76	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
77	assert_status "Login with invalid password returns 401" "401" "$HTTP_CODE"
78	assert_json_field "Invalid login returns error code" "$BODY" "['error']['code']" "ERR_INVALID_CREDENTIALS"
79	
80	# Test 4: Login with nonexistent user
81	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
82	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
83	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
84	    -d '{"username":"nonexistent","password":"testpass123"}')
85	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
86	assert_status "Login with nonexistent user returns 401" "401" "$HTTP_CODE"
87	
88	# Test 5: Login with missing fields
89	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
90	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
91	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
92	    -d '{"username":"admin"}')
93	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
94	assert_status "Login with missing password returns 422" "422" "$HTTP_CODE"
95	
96	# Test 6: Login with short username
97	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
98	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
99	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
100	    -d '{"username":"ab","password":"testpass123"}')
101	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
102	assert_status "Login with short username returns 422" "422" "$HTTP_CODE"
103	
104	# Test 7: Login with short password
105	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
106	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
107	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
108	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"short"}')
109	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
110	assert_status "Login with short password returns 422" "422" "$HTTP_CODE"
111	
112	# Test 8: Access protected endpoint without auth
113	echo ""
114	echo "--- Authorization Tests ---"
115	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
116	    -X GET "$BASE_URL/api/dashboard")
117	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
118	assert_status "Accessing protected endpoint without auth returns 401" "401" "$HTTP_CODE"
119	
120	# Test 9: Access protected endpoint with auth
121	CSRF_TOKEN=$(grep csrf_token "$COOKIE_JAR" | awk '{print $NF}')
122	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
123	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $CSRF_TOKEN" \
124	    -X GET "$BASE_URL/api/dashboard")
125	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
126	assert_status "Accessing dashboard with valid auth returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
127	
128	# Test 10: Refresh token
129	echo ""
130	echo "--- Token Refresh Tests ---"
131	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$COOKIE_JAR" -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
132	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $CSRF_TOKEN" \
133	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/refresh")
134	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
135	assert_status "Token refresh returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
136	
137	# Test 11: Refresh without cookie
138	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
139	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/refresh")
140	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
141	assert_status "Refresh without cookie returns 401" "401" "$HTTP_CODE"
142	
143	# Test 12: Logout
144	echo ""
145	echo "--- Logout Tests ---"
146	CSRF_TOKEN=$(grep csrf_token "$COOKIE_JAR" | awk '{print $NF}')
147	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
148	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $CSRF_TOKEN" \
149	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/logout")
150	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
151	assert_status "Logout returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
152	
153	# Test 13: Login with seller1
154	echo ""
155	echo "--- Role-specific Login Tests ---"
156	SELLER_JAR=$(mktemp)
157	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -c "$SELLER_JAR" \
158	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
159	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
160	    -d '{"username":"seller1","password":"testpass123"}')
161	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
162	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
163	assert_status "Login as seller1" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
164	assert_json_field "Seller1 username" "$BODY" "['user']['username']" "seller1"
165	rm -f "$SELLER_JAR"
166	
167	# Test 14: Login with buyer1
168	BUYER_JAR=$(mktemp)
169	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -c "$BUYER_JAR" \
170	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
171	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
172	    -d '{"username":"buyer1","password":"testpass123"}')
173	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
174	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
175	assert_status "Login as buyer1" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
176	assert_json_field "Buyer1 username" "$BODY" "['user']['username']" "buyer1"
177	rm -f "$BUYER_JAR"
178	
179	# ===========================================================
180	# Account lockout integration test
181	# Creates a disposable user, hammers it with 5 bad passwords,
182	# verifies the 6th attempt returns 423, then unlocks via admin.
183	# ===========================================================
184	echo ""
185	echo "--- Account Lockout Integration Test ---"
186	
187	# Log in as admin to create a throwaway user
188	LOCKOUT_ADMIN_JAR=$(mktemp)
189	curl -s -c "$LOCKOUT_ADMIN_JAR" \
190	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
191	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
192	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"testpass123"}' > /dev/null
193	LOCKOUT_ADMIN_CSRF=$(grep csrf_token "$LOCKOUT_ADMIN_JAR" | awk '{print $NF}')
194	
195	LOCKOUT_USER="lockout_test_$(date +%s)"
196	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$LOCKOUT_ADMIN_JAR" \
197	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
198	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $LOCKOUT_ADMIN_CSRF" \
199	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users" \
200	    -d "{\"username\":\"$LOCKOUT_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\",\"display_name\":\"Lockout Test\"}")
201	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
202	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
203	LOCKOUT_USER_ID=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
204	
205	if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "201" ] || [ -z "$LOCKOUT_USER_ID" ] || [ "$LOCKOUT_USER_ID" = "" ]; then
206	    echo "  SKIP: Could not create throwaway user for lockout test (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)"
207	else
208	    # Send 5 failed logins
209	    for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
210	        curl -s -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
211	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
212	            -d "{\"username\":\"$LOCKOUT_USER\",\"password\":\"wrongwrongwrong\"}" > /dev/null
213	    done
214	
215	    # 6th attempt — account should be locked (HTTP 423)
216	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
217	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
218	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
219	        -d "{\"username\":\"$LOCKOUT_USER\",\"password\":\"wrongwrongwrong\"}")
220	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
221	    BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
222	    assert_status "6th failed login returns 423 (account locked)" "423" "$HTTP_CODE"
223	    assert_json_field "Locked response error code" "$BODY" "['error']['code']" "ERR_ACCOUNT_LOCKED"
224	
225	    # Even correct password should be rejected while locked
226	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
227	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
228	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
229	        -d "{\"username\":\"$LOCKOUT_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}")
230	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
231	    assert_status "Correct password rejected while locked (423)" "423" "$HTTP_CODE"
232	
233	    # Admin unlocks the account
234	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$LOCKOUT_ADMIN_JAR" \
235	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
236	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $LOCKOUT_ADMIN_CSRF" \
237	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$LOCKOUT_USER_ID/unlock")
238	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
239	    assert_status "Admin unlocks account (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
240	
241	    # Now the correct password should work again
242	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
243	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
244	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
245	        -d "{\"username\":\"$LOCKOUT_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}")
246	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
247	    assert_status "Login succeeds after admin unlock (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
248	fi
249	rm -f "$LOCKOUT_ADMIN_JAR"
250	
251	# ===========================================================
252	# CSRF negative test
253	# Sends a protected POST with auth cookies but WITHOUT the
254	# X-CSRF-Token header and expects 403.
255	# ===========================================================
256	echo ""
257	echo "--- CSRF Negative Tests ---"
258	
259	# Get fresh cookies
260	CSRF_JAR=$(mktemp)
261	curl -s -c "$CSRF_JAR" \
262	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
263	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
264	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"testpass123"}' > /dev/null
265	
266	# POST without X-CSRF-Token header — should be rejected
267	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$CSRF_JAR" \
268	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/logout")
269	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
270	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
271	assert_status "POST /auth/logout without X-CSRF-Token returns 403" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
272	
273	CSRF_ERR_MSG=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "
274	import sys,json
275	try:
276	    d=json.load(sys.stdin)
277	    # Echo wraps HTTPError; try both shapes
278	    if 'error' in d:
279	        print(d['error'].get('message',''))
280	    elif 'message' in d:
281	        m = d['message']
282	        if isinstance(m, dict):
283	            print(m.get('error',{}).get('message',''))
284	        else:
285	            print(m)
286	    else:
287	        print('')
288	except: print('')
289	" 2>/dev/null)
290	if echo "$CSRF_ERR_MSG" | grep -qi "csrf"; then
291	    echo "  PASS: Error message mentions CSRF"
292	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
293	else
294	    echo "  FAIL: Error message should mention CSRF (got: '$CSRF_ERR_MSG')"
295	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
296	fi
297	
298	# PATCH without X-CSRF-Token — should also be rejected
299	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$CSRF_JAR" \
300	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
301	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/notifications/read-all")
302	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
303	assert_status "POST /notifications/read-all without CSRF returns 403" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
304	
305	# GET should pass without X-CSRF-Token (safe method, CSRF not checked)
306	CSRF_TOKEN_VAL=$(grep csrf_token "$CSRF_JAR" | awk '{print $NF}')
307	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$CSRF_JAR" \
308	    "$BASE_URL/api/dashboard")
309	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
310	assert_status "GET /dashboard without X-CSRF-Token returns 200 (safe method)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
311	
312	# POST with wrong CSRF value — should be rejected
313	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$CSRF_JAR" \
314	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: totally-wrong-token" \
315	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/logout")
316	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
317	assert_status "POST with wrong X-CSRF-Token returns 403" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
318	
319	rm -f "$CSRF_JAR"
320	
321	echo ""
322	echo "=== Auth Tests Summary: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed ==="
323	exit $FAIL
324

1	package router
2	
3	import (
4		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
5		"github.com/ledgermint/platform/internal/cache"
6		"github.com/ledgermint/platform/internal/handler"
7		"github.com/ledgermint/platform/internal/middleware"
8		"github.com/ledgermint/platform/internal/store"
9		"go.uber.org/zap"
10	)
11	
12	type Handlers struct {
13		Auth         *handler.AuthHandler
14		User         *handler.UserHandler
15		Collectible  *handler.CollectibleHandler
16		Order        *handler.OrderHandler
17		Message      *handler.MessageHandler
18		Notification *handler.NotificationHandler
19		Analytics    *handler.AnalyticsHandler
20		ABTest       *handler.ABTestHandler
21		Admin        *handler.AdminHandler
22	}
23	
24	func Setup(e *echo.Echo, h Handlers, signingKey []byte, userStore *store.UserStore, hotCache *cache.HotCache, logger *zap.Logger) {
25		// Global middleware
26		e.Use(middleware.RequestID())
27		e.Use(middleware.StructuredLogger(logger))
28		e.Use(middleware.IPFilter(userStore, hotCache))
29		e.Use(middleware.CSRF())
30	
31		api := e.Group("/api")
32	
33		// Auth (public)
34		loginRL := middleware.LoginRateLimiter()
35		auth := api.Group("/auth")
36		auth.POST("/login", h.Auth.Login, loginRL.Middleware())
37		auth.POST("/refresh", h.Auth.Refresh)
38	
39		// Protected routes
40		protected := api.Group("", middleware.JWTAuth(signingKey))
41	
42		// Auth (protected)
43		protected.POST("/auth/logout", h.Auth.Logout)
44	
45		// Dashboard
46		protected.GET("/dashboard", h.Analytics.GetDashboard)
47	
48		// Users (admin only)
49		adminRole := middleware.RequireRole("administrator")
50		users := protected.Group("/users", adminRole)
51		users.POST("", h.User.Create)
52		users.GET("", h.User.List)
53		users.GET("/:id", h.User.Get)
54		users.PATCH("/:id", h.User.Update)
55		users.POST("/:id/roles", h.User.AddRole)
56		users.DELETE("/:id/roles/:roleId", h.User.RemoveRole)
57		users.POST("/:id/unlock", h.User.Unlock)
58	
59		// Collectibles
60		listingRL := middleware.ListingRateLimiter()
61		collectibles := protected.Group("/collectibles")
62		collectibles.GET("", h.Collectible.List)
63		collectibles.GET("/mine", h.Collectible.ListMine, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
64		collectibles.GET("/:id", h.Collectible.Get)
65		collectibles.POST("", h.Collectible.Create, middleware.RequireRole("seller"), listingRL.Middleware())
66		collectibles.PATCH("/:id", h.Collectible.Update, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
67		collectibles.PATCH("/:id/hide", h.Collectible.Hide, adminRole)
68		collectibles.PATCH("/:id/publish", h.Collectible.Publish, adminRole)
69	
70		// Orders
71		orderRL := middleware.OrderRateLimiter()
72		orders := protected.Group("/orders")
73		orders.POST("", h.Order.Create, middleware.RequireRole("buyer"), orderRL.Middleware())
74		orders.GET("", h.Order.List)
75		orders.GET("/:id", h.Order.Get)
76		orders.POST("/:id/confirm", h.Order.Confirm, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
77		orders.POST("/:id/process", h.Order.Process, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
78		orders.POST("/:id/complete", h.Order.Complete, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
79		orders.POST("/:id/cancel", h.Order.Cancel)
80		orders.PATCH("/:id/fulfillment", h.Order.UpdateFulfillment, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
81	
82		// Messages
83		messageRL := middleware.MessageRateLimiter()
84		orders.GET("/:orderId/messages", h.Message.List)
85		orders.POST("/:orderId/messages", h.Message.Send, messageRL.Middleware())
86	
87		// Notifications
88		notifications := protected.Group("/notifications")
89		notifications.GET("", h.Notification.List)
90		notifications.PATCH("/:id/read", h.Notification.MarkRead)
91		notifications.POST("/read-all", h.Notification.MarkAllRead)
92		notifications.POST("/:id/retry", h.Notification.Retry)
93		notifications.GET("/preferences", h.Notification.GetPreferences)
94		notifications.PUT("/preferences", h.Notification.UpdatePreferences)
95	
96		// Analytics (admin + compliance)
97		analyticsRole := middleware.RequireRole("administrator", "compliance_analyst")
98		analytics := protected.Group("/analytics", analyticsRole)
99		analytics.GET("/funnel", h.Analytics.GetFunnel)
100		analytics.GET("/retention", h.Analytics.GetRetention)
101		analytics.GET("/content-performance", h.Analytics.GetContentPerformance)
102	
103		// A/B Tests (admin only)

1	package model
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"time"
6	
7		"github.com/google/uuid"
8	)
9	
10	type OrderStatus string
11	
12	const (
13		OrderStatusPending    OrderStatus = "pending"
14		OrderStatusConfirmed  OrderStatus = "confirmed"
15		OrderStatusProcessing OrderStatus = "processing"
16		OrderStatusCompleted  OrderStatus = "completed"
17		OrderStatusCancelled  OrderStatus = "cancelled"
18	)
19	
20	var AllowedTransitions = map[OrderStatus][]OrderStatus{
21		OrderStatusPending:    {OrderStatusConfirmed, OrderStatusCancelled},
22		OrderStatusConfirmed:  {OrderStatusProcessing, OrderStatusCancelled},
23		OrderStatusProcessing: {OrderStatusCompleted},
24		OrderStatusCompleted:  {},
25		OrderStatusCancelled:  {},
26	}
27	
28	func (s OrderStatus) CanTransitionTo(target OrderStatus) bool {
29		for _, allowed := range AllowedTransitions[s] {
30			if allowed == target {
31				return true
32			}
33		}
34		return false
35	}
36

38		ID                  uuid.UUID       `json:"id"`

46		CancelledBy         *uuid.UUID      `json:"cancelled_by,omitempty"`

89	    -d '{"preferences":{"order_confirmed":true,"order_cancelled":false}}')
90	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
91	assert_status "Update notification preferences returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
92	
93	# Test 5: Notifications require auth
94	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" "$BASE_URL/api/notifications")
95	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
96	assert_status "Notifications require authentication (401)" "401" "$HTTP_CODE"
97	
98	# --- Dashboard ---
99	echo ""
100	echo "--- Dashboard Tests ---"
101	
102	# Test 6: Dashboard returns expected fields
103	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
104	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
105	    "$BASE_URL/api/dashboard")
106	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
107	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
108	assert_status "Dashboard returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
109	
110	HAS_FIELDS=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "
111	import sys,json
112	d=json.load(sys.stdin)
113	print('open_orders' in d and 'owned_collectibles' in d and 'unread_notifications' in d)
114	" 2>/dev/null)
115	if [ "$HAS_FIELDS" = "True" ]; then
116	    echo "  PASS: Dashboard has expected fields"
117	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
118	else
119	    echo "  FAIL: Dashboard missing expected fields"
120	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
121	fi
122	
123	# --- Admin: Users ---
124	echo ""
125	echo "--- Admin User Management ---"
126	
127	# Test 7: List users as admin
128	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
129	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
130	    "$BASE_URL/api/users")
131	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
132	assert_status "List users as admin returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
133	
134	# Test 8: Create user as admin
135	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
136	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
137	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
138	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users" \
139	    -d '{"username":"testuser_api","password":"testpass123","display_name":"API Test User"}')
140	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
141	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
142	assert_status "Create user as admin returns 201" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
143	
144	TEST_USER_ID=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['id'])" 2>/dev/null)
145	
146	# Test 9: Add role to user
147	if [ -n "$TEST_USER_ID" ] && [ "$TEST_USER_ID" != "None" ]; then
148	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
149	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
150	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
151	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$TEST_USER_ID/roles" \
152	        -d '{"role_name":"buyer"}')
153	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
154	    assert_status "Add buyer role to user returns 200 or 201" "20" "${HTTP_CODE:0:2}"
155	fi
156	
157	# Test 10: Buyer cannot list users
158	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
159	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
160	    "$BASE_URL/api/users")
161	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
162	assert_status "Buyer cannot list users (403)" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
163	
164	# Test 11: Buyer cannot create user
165	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
166	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
167	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
168	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users" \
169	    -d '{"username":"shouldfail","password":"testpass123","display_name":"Fail"}')
170	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
171	assert_status "Buyer cannot create user (403)" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
172	
173	# --- Admin: Analytics ---
174	echo ""
175	echo "--- Analytics Access ---"
176	
177	# Test 12: Admin can access analytics funnel
178	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
179	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
180	    "$BASE_URL/api/analytics/funnel?days=7")
181	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
182	assert_status "Admin can access analytics funnel (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
183	
184	# Test 13: Admin can access retention
185	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
186	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
187	    "$BASE_URL/api/analytics/retention?days=30")
188	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
189	assert_status "Admin can access retention (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
190	
191	# Test 14: Admin can access content performance
192	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
193	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
194	    "$BASE_URL/api/analytics/content-performance")
195	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
196	assert_status "Admin can access content performance (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
197	
198	# Test 15: Buyer cannot access analytics
199	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
200	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
201	    "$BASE_URL/api/analytics/funnel")
202	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
203	assert_status "Buyer cannot access analytics (403)" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
204	
205	# Test 16: Compliance analyst CAN access analytics
206	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ANALYST_JAR" \
207	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ANALYST_CSRF" \
208	    "$BASE_URL/api/analytics/funnel?days=7")
209	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
210	assert_status "Compliance analyst can access analytics (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
211	
212	# --- Admin: IP Rules ---
213	echo ""
214	echo "--- Admin IP Rules ---"
215	
216	# Test 17: List IP rules
217	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
218	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
219	    "$BASE_URL/api/admin/ip-rules")
220	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
221	assert_status "List IP rules returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
222	
223	# Test 18: Admin can access anomalies
224	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
225	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
226	    "$BASE_URL/api/admin/anomalies")
227	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
228	assert_status "List anomalies returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
229	
230	# Test 19: Admin can access metrics
231	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
232	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
233	    "$BASE_URL/api/admin/metrics")
234	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
235	assert_status "Admin metrics returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
236	
237	# --- A/B Tests ---
238	echo ""
239	echo "--- A/B Test Endpoints ---"
240	
241	# Test 20: List AB tests
242	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
243	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
244	    "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests")
245	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
246	assert_status "List A/B tests returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
247	
248	# Test 21: Get assignments (available to all authenticated users)
249	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
250	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
251	    "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests/assignments")
252	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
253	assert_status "Get AB test assignments returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
254	
255	# Test 22: Buyer cannot list AB tests
256	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
257	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
258	    "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests")
259	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
260	assert_status "Buyer cannot list A/B tests (403)" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
261	
262	echo ""
263	echo "=== Notifications & Admin Tests Summary: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed ==="
264	exit $FAIL
265
